title: Obtaining Competent Cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a37f3","text":"Obtencion of 100ml competent E.coli aliquots that must be stored in -80C ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3vu2q","text":"Competent cells are fragile. All work must be done under a laminar flux cabin and in aseptic conditions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Incubate 10ml of E.coli BL21(DE3) or DH5α o/n without plasmid (from –80°C stock). To do that, add 10 mL of sterile LB media in a 50ml sterile falcon tube. With the use of a yellow micropipette, scratch the surface of the frozen E.coli from the -80°C freezer and toss it into the Falcon containing the media. This must be done fast, and the E.coli must not be outside the freezer more than 1 or 2 minutes (Use the termoblock from the -20°C to keep the E.coli stock frozen).
Re-inoculate in a new 10 mL LB. Incubate until absorbance is 0.6-0.7 OD550 02:00:00 .
Under the Laminar flux Cabin, throw the supernatant and resuspend in the same volume of CaCl2 100 millimolar (mM) cold and sterile. Do not use vortex!
Resuspend in 1/10th of the previous volume of cold and sterile CaCl2 and 15% glycerol. Aliquot them in 100 µL (using 1.5ml centrifuge tubes).
These aliquots can be used right away or can be stored in the -80°C freezer.
creator: Miquel Estévez-Gay
